title: 10 best herbal decoctions for insect bites
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for insect bites are a natural remedy that involves steeping herbs in hot water to create a soothing liquid extract. This concoction helps alleviate the discomfort and itching caused by insect bites, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Examples of herbal decoctions that provide relief from insect bites include peppermint, lavender, and chamomile. These decoctions can be applied topically or consumed as a tea, providing instant relief and improving overall well-being. For instance, a person who suffered from severe mosquito bites on a camping trip found relief by drinking a chamomile decoction, allowing them to enjoy the rest of their adventure without discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the plant reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with bug bites. The decoction's antimicrobial activity also prevents infection and promotes healing by reducing the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms on the skin. Additionally, pot marigold's analgesic properties provide relief from pain and discomfort caused by insect bites, making it a natural remedy for soothing irritated skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving discomfort.

Antiseptic: The plant's essential oils and flavonoids also exhibit antiseptic properties, helping to prevent infection and promote healing of the affected area, reducing the risk of complications from insect bites.

Antihistamine: Pot marigold contains compounds that can help alleviate allergic reactions caused by insect bites, such as itching and redness, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a sense of calm.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help reduce swelling, redness, and infection associated with insect bites, promoting faster healing.

Phenolic acids (specifically, caffeic and ferulic acids): These compounds exhibit strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help alleviate pain, itching, and inflammation caused by insect bites.

Carotenoids (specifically, beta-carotene): As a powerful antioxidant, beta-carotene helps protect the skin from oxidative damage, reducing inflammation and promoting the healing process after an insect bite.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Leaves: They are used because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that aid in reducing redness and swelling caused by insect bites.
Roots: They are used for their antimicrobial properties, which help prevent infection and promote healing of insect bite wounds.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 fresh or dried calendula flowers and clean them thoroughly with filtered water.
Combine the cleaned calendula flowers with 1 quart of water in a medium saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer for 20-25 minutes allowing the mixture to infuse and release its properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool before transferring it to a clean glass bottle for storage and future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort decoctions helps with insect bites because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction, which is made by steeping the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water, can be applied topically to reduce swelling and itching caused by bug bites. The active compound hyperforin has been shown to have analgesic and antihistamine effects, providing quick relief from insect bite discomfort. Additionally, St John's Wort decoctions may also help prevent infection and promote wound healing.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic property: St. John's Wort decoction has antiseptic properties, which help to prevent infection and reduce the risk of septicemia (blood poisoning) from insect bites.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in St. John's Wort decoctions can help to reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with insect bites, making them a natural remedy for itching and discomfort.

Antihistamine property: St. John's Wort contains antihistamines that help to block histamine receptors, reducing the allergic response to insect bites and alleviating symptoms such as itching, hives, and redness.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain, swelling, and itching associated with insect bites.
Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid that acts as an antihistamine, inhibiting the release of histamine, which causes allergic reactions, including itching, redness, and swelling, due to insect bites.
N-feruloyltyramine: N-feruloyltyramine has anti-inflammatory and antipruritic (anti-itch) effects, helping to alleviate itching, redness, and swelling caused by insect bites.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm irritated skin caused by insect bites.
Flowers: Used for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antihistamine properties, which aid in reducing itching, swelling, and redness from insect bites.
Roots: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce pain, swelling, and infection caused by insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop the plant material into smaller pieces using scissors to increase the surface area.
Combine 2-3 tablespoons of chopped plant material with 1 quart of boiling water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture for 5-10 minutes over low heat to extract the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mountain arnica decoctions helps with insect bites because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which quickly reduce swelling and redness. The decoction's antiseptic qualities also prevent infection by killing bacteria that can cause further irritation. Additionally, arnica's soothing effects calm itching and discomfort, allowing the skin to heal faster. As a result, herbal mountain arnica decoctions provide instant relief from insect bites, making them a natural and effective remedy for reducing discomfort and promoting rapid recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties: Mountain arnica decoctions help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with insect bites due to their ability to inhibit inflammatory mediators and block pain signals.

Antimicrobial properties: The decoction's antimicrobial activity helps prevent infection by killing or inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that may enter the bite wound through scratching or contamination.

Immune-stimulating and anti-allergic properties: Mountain arnica's immune-stimulating effects help modulate the body's response to insect bites, reducing the severity of allergic reactions and promoting a balanced immune response.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce inflammation and itching associated with insect bites by inhibiting the release of histamine and other pro-inflammatory mediators.

Triterpenes: Triterpenes present in Arnica montana, such as arnidiol and arnidiol-3-O-butyrate, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in soothing and calming irritated skin caused by insect bites.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Arnica montana, like caffeic acid and ferulic acid, exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to mitigate the redness, swelling, and discomfort caused by insect bites.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: They are used for their ability to reduce itching and swelling caused by insect bites.
Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce pain and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of fresh arnica montana flowers or 15-30 grams of dried flowers.
Chop the harvested arnica montana flowers into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped arnica montana flowers with 1 liter of water in a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower decoctions helps with insect bites because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, such as echinacoside and sesquiterpenes. These natural components work together to reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites, while also preventing infection and promoting healing. The decoction's antipruritic properties help alleviate discomfort and relieve the intense itching associated with bug bites, making it a soothing and effective solution for calming irritated skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Kansas coneflower decoctions contain flavonoids and polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites.
Antihistamine: The decoctions also possess antihistamine-like compounds that can block histamine receptors, alleviating symptoms such as redness, itching, and hives associated with insect bites.

Antibacterial: Kansas coneflower's antimicrobial properties can help prevent bacterial infections caused by contaminated insect bites, promoting wound healing and reducing the risk of secondary infections.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the swelling and redness associated with insect bites.
Flavonoids: As antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, flavonoids can help soothe the skin, reduce pain, and prevent secondary infections caused by insect bites.
Alkamides: These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of insect bites, such as pain, swelling, and redness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of alkaloids, particularly echinacoside, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their ability to provide a soothing and cooling effect when applied topically to insect bites, reducing itching and inflammation.
Flowers: The flowers are often used in decoctions for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce swelling and prevent infection in insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-4 grams of dried roots and 1 liter of cold water to prepare the herbal decoction.
Cut the dried roots into smaller pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine the cut roots with cold water in a saucepan and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat and let the decoction simmer for 10-15 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the roots and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain decoctions helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and analgesic properties. The decoction's active compounds, such as allantoin and cinnamic acid, help to reduce swelling and itching caused by insect stings or bites. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of plantain work to prevent infection and promote healing. This natural remedy soothes and calms the skin, providing quick relief from itchy and painful insect bites.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain decoctions help reduce swelling and redness caused by insect bites, thanks to its ability to inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes and reduce inflammation.
Antimicrobial properties: Plantain's antimicrobial properties help prevent infection and promote healing of insect bite wounds, reducing the risk of bacterial or fungal invasion.

Soothing and cooling properties: Plantain decoctions have a calming effect on the skin, providing relief from itching, burning, and stinging sensations associated with insect bites, while also helping to reduce heat and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Apolactones: Apolactones, a type of flavonoid glycoside, help with insect bites by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antihistaminic properties, which can reduce redness, itching, and swelling caused by insect bites.

Apolavin: Apolavin, a flavonoid glycoside, helps with insect bites by showing potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can protect the skin from oxidative damage and reduce the severity of insect bite symptoms.

Tannins: Tannins, a type of polyphenol, help with insect bites by exhibiting astringent properties, which can help to reduce swelling, itching, and redness caused by insect bites, and also aid in the healing of the affected area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Plantago major for decoctions due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds.
Stems: Stems are used in decoctions to take advantage of their astringent properties, which help to reduce swelling and itching from insect bites.
Roots: Roots are used in decoctions for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and protect the affected area from infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100 grams of dried plantago major leaves and flowers in the morning on a dry day.
Chop the harvested plantago major into small pieces to increase the surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped plantago major with 1 liter of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer the plantago major decoction for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to release.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean container to remove the plantago major solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle decoctions helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory, antihistamine, and antimicrobial properties. The decoction's soothing and cooling effect can reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites, providing instant relief. The antioxidants present in the nettle also help to neutralize the toxins released from the insect's saliva during biting, further reducing inflammation and promoting healing. By using stinging nettle decoctions, individuals can effectively alleviate discomfort and promote a speedy recovery from insect bites.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle decoctions have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling, redness, and itching caused by insect bites, allowing for faster healing.

Antihistamine properties: Nettle decoctions contain natural antihistamines that counteract the release of histamine, a chemical released during an allergic reaction to insect bites, which reduces itching and discomfort.
Astringent properties: Stinging nettle decoctions have astringent compounds that help dry out and contract the skin, reducing weeping and oozing caused by insect bites, and promoting faster healing.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Specifically, the alkaloids in Urtica dioica, such as stachydrine and histamine, help counteract the allergic responses triggered by insect bites, reducing inflammation and itching.
Phenolic acids (including folic acid and chlorogenic acid): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the pain and swelling associated with insect bites.
Polysaccharides (including pectin and rhamnogalacturonan): These complex carbohydrates can help soothe and protect the skin, reducing irritation and promoting the healing of insect bite lesions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part for their high content of histamine-releasing compounds, which help reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites.
Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort caused by insect bites.
Roots: The roots of the plant are used for their ability to reduce inflammation and itching, making them an effective ingredient in decoctions for treating insect bite symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest urtica dioica leaves and stems from a clean and unpolluted area in late spring or early summer.
Rinse the harvested urtica dioica plant material in a fine mesh sieve under cold running water.
Combine 1 cup of the rinsed urtica dioica plant material with 4 cups of water in a saucepan.
Bring the plant material and water mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine mesh sieve and discard the solids then serve the liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe decoctions helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing properties. The gel-like substance within aloe vera contains compounds such as aloin and aloe-emodin, which work together to reduce swelling and redness caused by insect bites. Additionally, the natural antibacterial agents in aloe help prevent infection and promote healing. By applying an aloe decoction topically, individuals can quickly alleviate discomfort, itching, and pain associated with insect bites, providing relief and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe decoctions contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which can help reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites.
Antimicrobial properties: Herbal aloe contains antimicrobial agents like salicylic acid and cinnamic acid that can help prevent bacterial or fungal infections from developing at the bite site.

Soothing and moisturizing properties: Aloe decoctions are rich in vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that can help soothe itchy skin, reduce redness, and promote skin hydration, making them an effective topical treatment for insect bites.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds help reduce inflammation and alleviate itching caused by insect bites by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone derivative has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm the skin affected by insect bites, reducing redness and swelling.
Beta-sitosterol: This phytosterol has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce the severity of allergic reactions to insect bites, such as redness, swelling, and itching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are rich in gel and other compounds that help soothe and reduce inflammation caused by insect bites.
Leaves (specifically the inner gel): The inner gel of Aloe vera leaves contains anti-inflammatory and anti-itch properties that help alleviate symptoms of insect bites.

Leaves (specifically the outer sap or latex): The outer sap or latex of Aloe vera leaves has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help combat infection and reduce swelling caused by insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves in the morning when they are at their most potent 500mg to 1000mg of leaf material is ideal.
Clean and peel the leaves to remove the outer green skin and the spiny edges to avoid irritation.
Chop the peeled leaves into small pieces and weigh them to ensure accurate dosing 500mg to 1000mg is recommended.
Boil the chopped aloe vera in 500ml of water for 10 to 15 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids to create a clear liquid herbal remedy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with insect bites because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When applied topically to affected areas, peppermint's cooling effect can reduce swelling and ease itching caused by insect stings or bites. Additionally, peppermint's antioxidant compounds help neutralize histamine release, which further reduces inflammation and discomfort. As a result, peppermint decoctions provide fast and effective relief from the itchy, painful symptoms associated with insect bites, making them a popular natural remedy for soothing skin irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint contains anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol, menthone, and limonene, which help reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by insect bites.

Antiseptic properties: The decoction's antiseptic properties help prevent infection by killing bacteria and other microorganisms that may have entered the bite wound through scratching or contact with contaminated surfaces.

Soothing and calming effects: Peppermint's menthol content can provide a cooling and soothing sensation on the skin, reducing discomfort and itching caused by insect bites, and promoting relaxation and calmness.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol, a terpene compound found in peppermint, helps with insect bites by cooling the skin, reducing itching and inflammation.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound found in peppermint, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate the symptoms of insect bites, such as redness and swelling.
Caryophyllene oxide: Caryophyllene oxide, a sesquiterpene found in peppermint, has anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties that help to soothe and calm the skin after an insect bite.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita are the most commonly used part to make decoctions for insect bites because they contain high concentrations of menthol and menthone, which provide anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita are used to make decoctions for insect bites due to their high content of volatile oils, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin.

Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita are used to make decoctions for insect bites because they contain a higher concentration of menthol and other compounds than the leaves, which makes them more effective at reducing inflammation and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh or dried mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the early morning.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped plant material with 500ml of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes or up to 30 minutes for a stronger infusion.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender decoctions helps with insect bites because it possesses anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties that work in harmony to soothe and calm irritated skin. The decoction's cooling effect reduces itching and swelling, while its antibacterial and antifungal properties prevent infection and promote wound healing. Additionally, lavender's calming aroma can also help to reduce stress and anxiety caused by the discomfort of insect bites, providing a sense of relaxation and relief.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Lavender's antibacterial properties help prevent bacterial infection from entering the wound, reducing the risk of further complications and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with insect bites, making them a soothing and calming remedy for itchy and inflamed skin.
Antihistamine: Lavender's antihistamine properties help alleviate itching, pruritus, and allergic reactions caused by insect bites, providing fast relief from uncomfortable symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene found in Lavandula angustifolia decoctions, linalool has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce swelling and prevent infection at the site of an insect bite.

Linalyl acetate: Another terpene in Lavandula angustifolia decoctions, linalyl acetate has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which help alleviate pain, itching, and redness associated with insect bites.
Lavandulol: A sesquiterpene alcohol in Lavandula angustifolia decoctions, lavandulol has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections, which can occur as a result of an insect bite.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Lavandula angustifolia are used to make decoctions for insect bites due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Flowers of Lavandula angustifolia are used to make decoctions for insect bites due to their soothing and calming effects.
Stems: Stems of Lavandula angustifolia are used to make decoctions for insect bites due to their ability to reduce itching and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency and aroma.
Grind the dried flowers into fine powder using a mortar and pestle to enhance bioavailability.
Combine 2-3 grams of the ground flowers with 100ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing properties. When applied topically to itchy bug bites, chamomile's gentle calmer cools the skin, reducing redness and swelling. Its natural astringent qualities also help to dry out weeping blisters, making them less prone to infection. Additionally, chamomile's mild antihistamine effects can alleviate itching and discomfort, providing quick relief from the annoyance of insect bites.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with insect bites, soothing irritated skin.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in chamomile decoctions, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation and damage caused by insect bites, promoting faster healing.

Calming and anti-pruritic property: Chamomile's soothing and calming effects on the skin help reduce itching and discomfort caused by insect bites, providing relief from the annoying sensations and allowing for a more comfortable healing process.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites.
Bisabolol: A sesquiterpene alcohol that possesses anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin affected by insect bites, reducing the risk of infection.
Chamazulene: A sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by insect bites, while also preventing infection.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Matricaria chamomilla are used to make decoctions for insect bites because of their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used in decoctions to help reduce itching and swelling caused by insect bites.
Roots: The roots of Matricaria chamomilla are used to make decoctions for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and heal insect bite wounds.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and measure out a dosage of 2-3 grams per 100ml of water.
Combine the dried flowers with 100ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the herb's active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Discard the solids and let the liquid cool before serving or storing in a sealed container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bitter compounds present in dandelion, such as taraxasterol and taraxanthin, have been shown to reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites. Additionally, the antioxidants in dandelion help to soothe and calm irritated skin, making it an effective natural remedy for reducing the discomfort and redness associated with insect bites. This makes dandelion decoctions a popular choice for many herbalists and natural health practitioners.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which can help reduce swelling and redness associated with insect bites, making it easier to manage discomfort.

Antihistamine effects: The decoction's flavonoids and triterpenoids have antihistamine properties, which can alleviate itching, burning, and hives caused by insect bites by blocking the release of histamine in the body.

Wound-healing and antibacterial properties: Dandelion contains antioxidants and compounds that promote wound healing, as well as exhibit antibacterial activity, helping to prevent infection and promote healthy tissue regeneration after an insect bite.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and preventing infection from insect bites.
Buds: The buds of the dandelion plant are rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and itching caused by insect bites, while also promoting healing and tissue repair.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried taraxacum officinale leaves or one teaspoon of fresh leaves.
Combine the gathered herbs with two cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain it through a fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Add one tablespoon of raw honey and stir until dissolved, then store the decoction in an airtight container.
Use the decoction as needed, applying it topically to the affected area or ingesting one quarter teaspoon three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel decoctions helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The astringent and soothing compounds in witch hazel, such as tannins and gallic acid, help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with bug bites. When applied topically, the decoction can calm irritated skin, promote healing, and even help to neutralize any venom or toxins released by the insect. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective way to alleviate discomfort and relieve the annoying symptoms of insect bites.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce swelling, redness, and itching caused by insect bites.

Phenolic Compounds: Phenolic compounds in Witch Hazel decoctions possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which help to combat infection and alleviate discomfort associated with insect bites.
Volatile Oils: The volatile oils present in Witch Hazel decoctions have a calming effect on the skin, reducing itching, redness, and inflammation caused by insect bites.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of tannins, which help to reduce inflammation and itching caused by insect bites.
Barks: The barks contain salicylic acid, similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hamamelis virginiana leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Chop 1/4 cup of hamamelis virginiana plant material into small pieces using scissors or a knife.
Combine chopped plant material with 8 ounces of cold water in a large glass jar.
Steep mixture for 20-30 minutes at room temperature or simmer gently on low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Strain decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids and store in refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with insect bites because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The decoction's flavonoids and terpenes work together to reduce swelling, itching, and redness caused by bug bites. Additionally, the soothing properties of maidenhair tree help calm irritated skin, reducing the urge to scratch and further exacerbate the bite. As a result, this natural remedy can provide quick relief from insect bites, allowing you to enjoy the outdoors without discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Help to reduce itching, swelling, and redness associated with insect bites by blocking histamine release from mast cells.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Reduce pain, inflammation, and edema caused by insect bites by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.
Antimicrobial properties: Prevent infection by killing bacteria, viruses, and fungi that may enter the wound or bite site, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are good for making decoctions because they contain anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin caused by insect bites.
Barks: The barks are effective in treating insect bites due to their antibacterial and antifungal properties, which help prevent infection and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a reputable source.
Measure one tablespoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves for every cup of water.
Bring one cup of water to a boil in a pot then add the measured ginkgo biloba leaves.
Allow the mixture to steep for 15 minutes and then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve.
Cool the decoction to room temperature before applying topically as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow decoctions helps with insect bites because they provide a soothing and anti-inflammatory relief to affected areas. The marshmallow herb contains mucilages, which create a protective barrier on the skin, reducing redness, swelling, and itching caused by bug bites. Additionally, marshmallow decoctions have antiseptic properties, helping to prevent infection and promote healing of minor wounds. By applying a topical application of marshmallow decoction to insect bites, individuals can find quick and natural relief from discomfort and irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow root contains mucilages that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling caused by insect bites.
Antibacterial: The antimicrobial properties of marshmallow root help prevent infection and promote healing of insect bite wounds, reducing the risk of bacterial or fungal growth.
Emollient: Marshmallow root's emollient properties moisturize and soften skin, relieving itching and discomfort associated with insect bites and helping to reduce the formation of blisters or hives.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and calm skin irritations caused by insect bites.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and itching associated with insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried marshmallow root, carefully selecting high-quality organic material.
Measure 200ml of boiling water and pour it over the dried roots in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the althaea officinalis to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids.
Store the prepared decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days, ready for topical application.

Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides in Urtica dioica have anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating effects, which can help to reduce the body's allergic response to insect bites and alleviate symptoms such as itching and swelling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Urtica dioica are commonly used due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory and antihistamine compounds.
Stems: The stems are also used, as they contain similar compounds to the leaves, providing relief from itching and inflammation.
Roots: The roots are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin affected by insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 cups of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems from a trusted source or grow your own.
Chop the urtica dioica leaves and stems into small pieces to release their medicinal properties.
Combine the chopped urtica dioica with 2 cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the urtica dioica to infuse into the water.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the urtica dioica solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata decoctions helps with insect bites because it has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's active compounds, such as aucubin and aucuparin, have been shown to reduce swelling and redness, while also preventing bacterial infections that can occur after an insect bite. Additionally, the decoction's soothing effects can calm the skin, reducing discomfort and itching associated with insect bites, providing quick relief and promoting a faster healing process.
remedy_constituents: Apolypodol: This constituent helps to reduce inflammation and itching associated with insect bites by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties.
Apolypodol 2: This constituent also aids in alleviating insect bite symptoms by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties.

Flavonoids (including aucubin): These constituents help to reduce inflammation and itching by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, as well as by inhibiting the release of histamine and other inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and reduce itching and swelling caused by insect bites.
Roots: They are used because of their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve pain and reduce inflammation associated with insect bites.
Stems: They are used due to their antiseptic and astringent properties, which help to prevent infection and reduce swelling caused by insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of the aerial parts of plantago lanceolata on a dry day after the first frost.
Chop the harvested plant parts into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Combine the chopped plant parts with 500ml of water in a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat then reduce the heat to low for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale decoctions help with insect bites because they have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's active compounds, such as symphytumine and allantoin, soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling. The decoction's antimicrobial action also helps to prevent infection and promote healing of the affected area. Additionally, the cooling and numbing effects of Symphytum officinale can provide quick relief from itching and discomfort caused by insect bites, providing a calming and soothing solution for irritated skin.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with insect bites by acting on the nervous system to minimize discomfort.
Saponins: Saponins in Symphytum officinale have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm the skin and reduce redness caused by insect bites.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in this herb possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing swelling and pain caused by insect bites, as well as promoting healing of the affected skin area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Symphytum officinale are used to make decoctions for insect bites due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of Symphytum officinale are used to make decoctions for insect bites due to their antiseptic and wound-healing properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Symphytum officinale are used to make decoctions for insect bites due to their soothing and calming effects on the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh symphytum officinale roots with clean scissors to prevent contamination.
Wash the roots with cold water to remove any dirt or debris thoroughly.
Chop the roots into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Steep 10g of the chopped roots in 500ml of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth into a clean container to remove solids completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago major decoctions helps with insect bites because they are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites, providing relief and promoting healing. The decoction also has antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infection and speed up the recovery process, making it a natural and effective remedy for soothing insect bites.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help with insect bites by reducing inflammation and alleviating itching due to their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties.
Flavonoids: They help with insect bites by exerting antiallergic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to calm down the skin and reduce itching and redness.
Apolysaccharides: They help with insect bites by promoting wound healing and reducing inflammation, which can aid in the recovery process from insect bites.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce itching and swelling caused by insect bites.
Roots: The roots are used for their antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which help to prevent infection and promote healing of the affected area.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to reduce itching, redness, and swelling caused by insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried aerial parts of the plant at the end of summer when flowers have formed.
Dry the harvested plant in a warm place with good air circulation for 7 to 10 days.
Chop 1 teaspoon of dried plant into small pieces to increase its surface area for infusion.
Combine chopped plant with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant container and let it steep for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove plant material and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis decoctions helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloe-emodin and aloin, which work together to reduce redness and swelling caused by insect bites. The decoction also has antiseptic properties, helping to prevent infection and promote healing of the affected area. This makes Aloe barbadensis decoctions a popular natural remedy for relieving discomfort and promoting recovery from insect bites.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and itching associated with insect bites.
Catechins: Catechins possess antimicrobial activity, which prevents the infection of insect-bite wounds and promotes faster healing.
Ursolic acid: Ursolic acid exhibits anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, helping to soothe itching and reduce inflammation caused by insect bites.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Aloe barbadensis are widely used due to their anti-inflammatory properties and soothing effects on skin irritations caused by insect bites.

Gel from Leaves: The clear gel inside the leaves of Aloe barbadensis is used as it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce itching, redness, and swelling caused by insect bites.

Sap from Leaves: The sap or juice from the leaves of Aloe barbadensis is used due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to promote healing, reduce pain, and prevent infection from insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200-300 grams of fresh aloe barbadensis leaves from a clean and disease-free plant.
Wash the harvested leaves with cold water to remove dirt and impurities thoroughly.
Chop the washed leaves into smaller pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Steep the chopped leaves in 1 liter of boiling water for 30-40 minutes to extract active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
